root_cause.summary: Controller response envelope was refactored from { user: ... } to { data: ... } but the test assertions were not updated to match the new response shape.
root_cause.detail: The commit 'refactor: standardise API response envelope to use { data } key' changed userController.js to return responses with a 'data' key instead of 'user'. Three tests in userController.test.js still assert against the old 'user' key, causing failures due to undefined property access.

failing_tests.name: should return user data
failing_tests.explanation: response.body.user is undefined because the controller now returns { data: user } instead of { user: user }.

failing_tests.name: should return 404 for missing user
failing_tests.explanation: The response object has a 'data' key instead of 'user'. The toEqual deep-equality check fails because the key names differ.

failing_tests.name: should update user
failing_tests.explanation: response.body.user is undefined, so accessing .name throws a TypeError. The test should read response.body.data.name.

fix.description: Replace all occurrences of 'response.body.user' with 'response.body.data' and update toHaveProperty('user') to toHaveProperty('data') and the toEqual object key from 'user' to 'data' in the three failing test cases.
fix.note: The controller (userController.js) is correct and should NOT be modified. Only the test file needs updating.
